mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 5-year rolling window, or Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that compress corporate earnings expectations, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns remain positive in over 65% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.5pp, or the signal loses statistical significance (p > 0.05) in a t-test.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based domestic demand growth that supports corporate revenue forecasts, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint positive signal yields negative 60-day forward returns in more than 55% of cases, or the interaction term loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.03) over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp contractions in the index of industrial production trigger immediate supply chain risk premiums that depress equity prices, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in over 60% of instances following INDPRO declines, or the mechanism fails to generate a significant alpha (t-stat < 1.5) during post-shock periods.

mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates combined with falling nonfarm payrolls create a stagflationary policy response that compresses equity multiples, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal produces positive 20-day returns in over 50% of triggered months, or the interaction effect shows no incremental predictive value over single-series models (F-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth falling below 1.0% annualized indicates severe demand weakness that forces earnings downgrades, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are positive in more than 55% of quarters where GDPC1 growth falls below 1.0%, or the signal's Rank IC drops below 0.02 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Increases in the consumer price index reflect higher inflation expectations that prompt immediate portfolio rebalancing away from equities, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no negative correlation with CPIAUCSL increases over a 5-year rolling period, or the signal fails to beat a buy-and-hold benchmark (Sharpe ratio < 0).

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment alongside contracting industrial production confirms a broad economic slowdown that reduces corporate profit margins, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields positive 20-day returns in over 60% of cases, or the combined predictor shows no statistical edge (p > 0.05) compared to baseline market returns.
